steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The first sentence establishes Still's importance and fame—he earned the moniker “the Father of the Underground Railroad.” The second sentence then presents a contradiction: “despite the fame of his contributions during his lifetime, Still is discussed far less today.” The word “despite” signals an unexpected contrast between his historical prominence and current obscurity.

steps.title: Choose the transition that bridges the contrast
steps.content: ”However” signals a thought that contradicts what came before, bridging the contrast between Still's historical prominence and his relative obscurity today. The other three choices either introduce examples, narrow focus, or suggest similarity—none of which fits a contrasting movement. That matches choice B — B is correct.
